```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-25
5	---
6	
7	# Domain strategy for nasapicture.com
8	
9	nasapicture.com is available. Planned structure:
10	
11	- `nasapicture.com` → static splash/landing page (Cloudflare Pages)
12	- `api.nasapicture.com` → the Worker (developer-facing)
13	- `get.nasapicture.com` → same Worker (human-friendly alias for sharing)
14	
15	Both subdomains point to the same Worker via CNAME. Landing page is a separate Cloudflare Pages project that embeds the image via `<img src="https://api.nasapicture.com">`.
16	
17	`get.` subdomain exists so non-technical people can intuitively understand the URL.
18	
19	Temp testing domain before purchase: nasapic.grantkeller.dev
20
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-26
5	---
6	
7	# Landing page for nasapicture.com
8	
9	Static landing page hosted on Cloudflare Pages, living in `landing/` subdirectory of this repo.
10	
11	## Plan
12	- Single `index.html` in `landing/`
13	- APOD image as hero via `<img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/">`
14	- Brief explanation of what the proxy is
15	- Endpoint listing
16	- Credit/link to Grant Keller
17	- Dark theme (space photos)
18	- Engineering-forward tone with personality (similar to /about endpoint)
19	
20	## Deploy
21	- Connect repo to Cloudflare Pages
22	- Set build output directory to `landing/`
23	- Assign `nasapicture.com` as custom domain
24
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 05:14:28Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0003_landing-page-critique.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-26
5	---
6	
7	# Landing page design critique (gallery revision)
8	
9	## AI Slop Verdict: Pass
10	
11	The gallery concept is a specific point of view, not a template. Restraint is genuine.
12	
13	## What's Working
14	
15	1. **Image presentation** — no frame, just shadow. Works with any APOD. Caption reads like a gallery placard.
16	2. **Usage row** — prose flowing into mono URLs feels natural. Copy buttons at 50% opacity are correctly subordinate.
17	3. **Endpoint list** — route/description/content-type is clean. Content-type column is a genuinely useful detail.
18	
19	## Priority Issues
20	
21	### 1. Info section has no visual boundary
22	Gallery hero ends at usage row, then "Endpoints" floats in the same void. The `.divider` element exists in CSS but was removed from HTML. Add it back, or give `.info` a subtle background (`--void-up`) to create a distinct zone.
23	
24	### 2. Caption width is unconstrained
25	Caption stretches full-width but image sizes to content. On wide viewports with tall/narrow APODs, the caption will be wider than the image, breaking the placard illusion. Wrap img + caption in a container with `display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column` so caption matches image width.
26	
27	### 3. H1 in header disappears on mobile
28	The three-column header flex squeezes the h1 on narrow viewports. No mobile-specific rule exists. Either hide it with sr-only on mobile or let the header wrap. Test at 375px.
29	
30	### 4. Endpoint rows lack touch padding
31	`padding: 0.75rem 0` is too tight for mobile tap targets (need 44px). Add horizontal padding and increase vertical on mobile.
32	
33	## Minor Issues
34	
35	- No favicon
36	- Reduced-motion selector references `.usage` but class is now `.usage-row`
37	- Second copy button `data-copy` uses HTML entities — clipboard will copy literal `&lt;` not `<`
38	- `NASApicture` capitalization doesn't match `<title>` and OG tags
39	- Portrait-orientation APODs will leave large empty space on sides (acceptable for gallery metaphor?)
40
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 05:14:29Z)**_

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-30
5	---
6	
7	# Add individual metadata routes
8	
9	Add dedicated routes for individual APOD metadata fields, so consumers can fetch just what they need without parsing JSON from `/info`:
10	
11	- `/date` — today's APOD date
12	- `/title` — image title
13	- `/explanation` — description text
14	- `/copyright` — copyright holder (if any)
15	
16	Returns plain text. Useful for shell scripts, widgets, and other lightweight integrations.
17
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 05:14:29Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0006_add-why-is-this-needed-section.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Add a "Why Is This Needed" Section That Explains the APOD API
8	
9	Add a section to the landing page that explains what the APOD API is, why it exists, and why a proxy like nasapicture.com is useful.
10	
11	## What to cover
12	
13	- **What is APOD?** — NASA publishes a new Astronomy Picture of the Day every day, with a professional explanation written by astronomers. It's been running since 1995.
14	- **The problem** — NASA's API requires an API key, has rate limits (30 req/hr on DEMO_KEY), sometimes returns videos instead of images, and doesn't offer a single stable URL that always points to "today's image."
15	- **What this solves** — nasapicture.com gives you a fixed URL that always returns today's image. No API key, no rate limits (edge-cached), no video days (auto-fallback). One URL, drop it in an `<img>` tag.
16	
17	## Tone & placement
18	
19	- Keep it concise — a few short paragraphs, not a wall of text. Match the existing voice (clever, minimal, precise).
20	- Place it between the hero image and the developer-facing route docs, so non-technical visitors get context before the technical details.
21	- Consider a heading like "Why does this exist?" or "The problem with APOD" — something that invites curiosity.
22
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 05:14:29Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0007_add-turn-off-the-lights-dim-mode.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Add a "Turn Off the Lights" Icon for Semi-Isolated Image Viewing
8	
9	Add a toggle icon that dims everything on the landing page except the APOD hero image. Creates a semi-isolated viewing experience — visitors can focus on the image without navigating away or opening it in a new tab.
10	
11	## Behavior
12	
13	- **Toggle on**: All surrounding UI (text, nav, starfield, glows) fades to near-black. The hero image remains at full brightness/opacity. A subtle vignette or dark overlay covers everything else.
14	- **Toggle off**: Click/tap the icon again, press Escape, or click anywhere outside the image to restore the normal view.
15	- Transition should be smooth (~300–400ms fade) and respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
16	
17	## Icon & placement
18	
19	- Position the icon near the hero image — top-right corner overlay or just below the image.
20	- Icon options: a simple lightbulb outline, a circle-half (like a dim switch), or an eye icon. Should be immediately recognizable without a label.
21	- On hover/focus, show a tooltip: "Focus mode" or "Dim surroundings."
22	
23	## Implementation notes
24	
25	- Pure CSS overlay approach preferred (no JS framework needed beyond toggling a class/data attribute).
26	- The image should stay in place in the DOM — don't reposition or clone it. Just layer a dark overlay behind it with a higher z-index on the image.
27	- Consider whether the starfield canvas should pause or just be hidden behind the overlay.
28
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 05:14:30Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0008_generate-daily-og-image.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Generate a daily OG image
8	
9	Generate a new Open Graph image every day, resized and cropped to the optimal 1200x630px dimensions. Adding a text overlay with the logo would be a bonus.
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	The current OG image points to `https://api.nasapicture.com/image.jpg`, which serves the raw APOD at whatever dimensions NASA provides (often square or portrait). Social platforms expect 1200x630px for optimal previews.
14	
15	## Recommended approach: `workers-og`
16	
17	[`workers-og`](https://github.com/kvnang/workers-og) renders HTML/CSS to PNG entirely inside a Worker (Satori → SVG → PNG, no browser needed). This is purpose-built for OG image generation at the edge.
18	
19	**How it would work:**
20	1. Add a `/og` route to the existing Worker (or a separate one)
21	2. Fetch today's APOD metadata from `/info` (title, date)
22	3. Render an HTML template with the APOD photo as background, title text, and nasapicture.com branding
23	4. Cache the result aggressively (24h TTL keyed to date) — one cold generation per day
24	5. Update the `og:image` meta tag to point to the new `/og` route
25	
26	**Pros:** Single Worker, zero external dependencies, familiar HTML/CSS for layout, no billing (stays within free Workers tier since we're generating the image ourselves, not using Cloudflare's Images API).
27	
28	**Cons:** Satori supports flexbox only (no CSS grid). Custom fonts need to be embedded as ArrayBuffers (adds to bundle size). Subset of CSS supported.
29	
30	## Alternative: Cloudflare Images binding
31	
32	Use the Images binding (`env.IMAGES`) to resize/crop the APOD to 1200x630 natively, then `.draw()` a pre-rendered text overlay PNG on top.
33	
34	- Requires `[images] binding = "IMAGES"` in `wrangler.toml`
35	- Free tier: 5,000 unique transformations/month (we'd use ~30/month — well within limits)
36	- Higher-fidelity resizing with smart crop/gravity options
37	- More moving parts (two-step: resize + composite)
38	
39	This is the upgrade path if `workers-og` quality isn't sufficient or if we need smarter cropping (e.g. face/subject detection).
40	
41	## Not viable
42	
43	- **Sharp** — uses native Node.js modules, incompatible with Workers
44	- **Canvas APIs** — no browser context in Workers
45	
46	## Useful links
47	
48	- [workers-og](https://github.com/kvnang/workers-og)
49	- [@cf-wasm/photon](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cf-wasm/photon) — WASM image processing if more control is needed
50	- [Cloudflare Images: draw overlays](https://developers.cloudflare.com/images/transform-images/draw-overlays/)
51	- [Cloudflare Images pricing](https://developers.cloudflare.com/images/pricing/) — free up to 5,000 transformations/month
52
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 05:14:30Z)**_

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Add Eastern Time explanation tooltip on date
8	
9	The APOD date in the hero caption can appear to be "tomorrow" for users in western US time zones (e.g. PT), since NASA publishes the APOD on Eastern Time. Add a subtle tooltip or hover note on the date explaining this.
10	
11	## Ideas
12	
13	- Tooltip on hover over the date (e.g. "NASA publishes on Eastern Time")
14	- Small inline note like "ET" next to the date
15	- A `title` attribute on the `<time>` element as the simplest approach
16
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-01 05:14:30Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0010_auto-refresh-landing-page-on-rollover.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Auto-refresh landing page on APOD rollover
8	
9	If a user leaves the landing page open, the image and caption should update automatically when NASA publishes the new APOD — no manual reload required.
10	
11	## Considerations
12	
13	- NASA publishes around midnight Eastern, but the exact time varies (sometimes delayed)
14	- Polling `/info` periodically and comparing the date to what's currently displayed is the simplest approach
15	- A reasonable interval might be every 15–30 minutes — frequent enough to catch the rollover without hammering the API (which is cached on the Worker side anyway)
16	- When a new date is detected: update the image `src`, caption title, and date, with a fade transition
17	- Should only poll when the tab is visible (`document.visibilityState`) to avoid wasting requests in background tabs
18
```
</details></tool-use>

-- Engineering-forward tone with personality (similar to /about endpoint)
-
-## Deploy
-- Connect repo to Cloudflare Pages
-- Set build output directory to `landing/`
-- Assign `nasapicture.com` as custom domain

----
-type: note
-status: inbox
-created: 2026-03-26
----
-
-# Landing page design critique (gallery revision)
-
-## AI Slop Verdict: Pass
-
-The gallery concept is a specific point of view, not a template. Restraint is genuine.
-
-## What's Working
-
-1. **Image presentation** — no frame, just shadow. Works with any APOD. Caption reads like a gallery placard.
-2. **Usage row** — prose flowing into mono URLs feels natural. Copy buttons at 50% opacity are correctly subordinate.
-3. **Endpoint list** — route/description/content-type is clean. Content-type column is a genuinely useful detail.
-
-## Priority Issues
-
-### 1. Info section has no visual boundary
-Gallery hero ends at usage row, then "Endpoints" floats in the same void. The `.divider` element exists in CSS but was removed from HTML. Add it back, or give `.info` a subtle background (`--void-up`) to create a distinct zone.
-
-### 2. Caption width is unconstrained
-Caption stretches full-width but image sizes to content. On wide viewports with tall/narrow APODs, the caption will be wider than the image, breaking the placard illusion. Wrap img + caption in a container with `display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column` so caption matches image width.
-
-### 3. H1 in header disappears on mobile
-The three-column header flex squeezes the h1 on narrow viewports. No mobile-specific rule exists. Either hide it with sr-only on mobile or let the header wrap. Test at 375px.
-
-### 4. Endpoint rows lack touch padding
-`padding: 0.75rem 0` is too tight for mobile tap targets (need 44px). Add horizontal padding and increase vertical on mobile.
-
-## Minor Issues
-
-- No favicon
-- Reduced-motion selector references `.usage` but class is now `.usage-row`
-- Second copy button `data-copy` uses HTML entities — clipboard will copy literal `&lt;` not `<`
-- `NASApicture` capitalization doesn't match `<title>` and OG tags
-- Portrait-orientation APODs will leave large empty space on sides (acceptable for gallery metaphor?)

-
-# Add Eastern Time explanation tooltip on date
-
-The APOD date in the hero caption can appear to be "tomorrow" for users in western US time zones (e.g. PT), since NASA publishes the APOD on Eastern Time. Add a subtle tooltip or hover note on the date explaining this.
-
-## Ideas
-
-- Tooltip on hover over the date (e.g. "NASA publishes on Eastern Time")
-- Small inline note like "ET" next to the date
-- A `title` attribute on the `<time>` element as the simplest approach
